info.name: Event Manager API v1
info.description:
Event Manager API is responsible for real-time event management and events history in Acronis Cyber Protect Cloud.

Guide

[https://developer.acronis.com/doc/outbound/apis/api-library/events/index.html](https://developer.acronis.com/doc/outbound/apis/api-library/events/index.html)

Full Reference

[https://developer.acronis.com/doc/events/v1/reference](https://developer.acronis.com/doc/events/v1/reference)

item.name: OAuth 2.0
item.description:
## Overview OAuth 2.0 security scheme definition for a service authorization. Supported grant types: - `password` - `client_credentials` - `authorization_code` - `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer` - `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code` Authorization URI example: `https://dev-cloud.acronis.com/api/2/idp/authorize` Access token URI example: `https://dev-cloud.acronis.com/api/2/idp/token` ## Request parameters ### Headers | Name | Description | | --- | --- | | `Authorization` | (Required) OAuth 2.0 access token.

**Type**: String
**Pattern**: ^Bearer \[0-9a-zA-Z-._\~+/\]+={0,2}$ | ## Scopes - `urn:acronis.com:event_manager:{cti_query}:publisher` - `urn:acronis.com:event_manager:{cti_query}:subscriber` - `urn:acronis.com:audit:{role|permissions}`

query.description:
(Required) Required, the position to fetch the offset for.

**Type**: String
**Allowed values**:
- `EARLIEST`
- `TIMESTAMP`
- `CURRENT`

(Optional) Required for timestamp positions. Fetch the offset position to the neares future from a specific time. The timestamp is specified in ISO 8601 format using the RFC 3339 profile.

**Type**: DateTime

request.description: Fetch the latest offset for a specific topic, providing a global view of the current stream state.

exec:
schema: {"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","$ref":"#/definitions/schema","definitions":{"schema":{"type":"array","items":{"description":"Event topic is a stream of events of the same or different types sharing the same delivery, ordering, consolidation & retention rules.
","type":"object","additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"id":{"description":"The topic id is an identifier of a stream of events in publishing and subscribe semantics","type":"string","pattern":"^cti\.([a-z][a-z0-9_]*\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*\.[a-z_][a-z0-9_.]*\.v[\d]+\.[\d]+)(~([a-z][a-z0-9_]*\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*\.[a-z_][a-z0-9_.]*\.v[\d]+\.[\d]+))*(~[0-9a-f]{8}\b-[0-9a-f]{4}\b-[0-9a-f]{4}\b-[0-9a-f]{4}\b-[0-9a-f]{12})?$","maxLength":1024},"description":{"description":"description of topic purpose and usage","type":"string"},"persistency":{"description":"true - events are persisted by Event Manager, Consumers will be able to fetch events that happened before the subscription
       (see the `archive_consolidation` and retention parameters)
false - events are not persisted and ordering is not strict. Consumers will see only events that happened
        after consumer created subscription in the Event Manager (Delivery Manager service).
        Events can be lost in Event Manager in case of restarts or if consumer was not able to receive events within
        some reasonable time interval and events retention happened in the Event Delivery manager
","type":"boolean"},"ordering":{"description":"PRODUCER     - Events must be ordered by Producer, Ingest Manager only validates event sequence id and ensures there are no lost events in the stream.
               The 'producer' mode is supposed to be used if producer can fully control events sequence, typically it means all the 'producer'
               instances are sharing the same database.

EVENTMANAGER - events are ordered by Event Manager (Ingest Manager). This mode is useful for multi-Producer case
               when there is no single Producer' database and so there is no way to guarantee events ordering across multiple Producers

NONE         - No events ordering. Suitable for audit-kind events from different sources
","enum":["NONE","PRODUCER","EVENTMANAGER"],"type":"string"},"producer_consolidation":{"description":"The `producer_consolidation` attribute reflects events consolidation mode on the Producer side, i.e. actual behavior
of the service rather than configurable parameter:

NONE - no consolidation happens and events are stored independently only

TTS - in addition to raw independent events there will be one more stream of events consolidated by type, tenantid
      and subject so the latest event with the same type, tenantid and subject will be stored without any retention

For topics that contain notification events reflecting state changes of long-lived objects (such as tenants, users,
settings, workloads, policies, etc.), it is typical ho have `producer_consolidation` set to `TTS`. Such configuration
allows the event stream to serve dual purposes: notifying state changes and enabling full event stream replay
(reconciliation) by clients.

For short-lived objects that have their own retention policies, such as tasks, activities, alerts and such,
it is typical to have `producer_consolidation` set to `NONE` and `retention` similar to the retention time
of the event origin object.
","enum":["NONE","TTS"],"type":"string"},"archive_consolidation":{"description":"The `archive_consolidation` attribute defines events consolidation mode on the Event Archive side:

NONE - no consolidation happens and events are stored independently only with appropriate retention

TTS  - event with the same type (T), tenantid (T) and subject (S) will be stored without any retention
       (i.e. will overwrite prev event with the same type, tenantid and subject eventually)

The `TTS` `archive_consolidation` can be typically used for topics that contain notification events reflecting
state changes of long-lived objects (such as tenants, users, settings, workloads, policies, etc.). It would
allow the event stream to serve dual purposes: notifying state changes and enabling full event stream replay
(reconciliation) by clients.

For short-lived objects that have their own retention policies, such as tasks, activities, alerts and such,
it is advisable to set `archive_consolidation` to `NONE` and `retention` similar to the retention time
of the event origin object.
","enum":["NONE","TTS"],"type":"string"},"retention":{"description":"Specifies the retention period for events within this topic on event archive side.

The retention duration begins from the moment an event is received by the Event Manager. Events older than the
specified duration are eligible for deletion by the retention job. Must be a valid ISO 8601 duration or
'INFINITE'.

This property is ignored if `archive_consolidation` is set to `TTS`, in this case retention effectively is
equal to 'INFINITE'

It's recommended to have retention period comparable to the object's lifetime to enable clients to use a single
mechanism for both change state notifications and full event stream replay (reconciliation).
","type":"string"}},"required":["id","description","persistency","ordering","producer_consolidation","archive_consolidation","retention"]}}}}

","type":"string","format":"date-time"},"ingest_time":{"description":"Time when the event was received by Event Manager and fully controlled by the Event Manager. The time should be in UTC and formatted as ISO 8601.
","type":"string","format":"date-time"},"previous":{"description":"Identifies the sequence number of the directly preceding event in the ordered topic stream. This field is used to explicitly link events in sequence, facilitating tracking of event order and detection of any missing events.
This field is optional for unordered topics (e.g. topic.ordering == NONE)
","type":"integer"},"sequence":{"description":"A unique identifier that represents the position of this event within the overall event stream. This sequence number is crucial for maintaining the order of events within ordered topics.
In 'PRODUCER' ordering mode, this number is assigned by the event producer.
In 'EVENTMANAGER' ordering mode, this number is assigned by the event manager.
This field is optional for unordered topics (e.g. topic.ordering == NONE)
","type":"integer"},"source":{"description":"The source that produced the event, specified by type and ID. It can be any context filled by Producer.

","type":"string","maxLength":1024},"subject":{"description":"ID of the specific object where event (e.g. state change notification) happened. For example, it can be a task id in the cti.a.p.em.event.v1.0~a.p.task.created.v1.0 event

query.description:
(Required) Required. Identifier of the subscription under which events are being consumed.  Used in conjunction with instance_id to manage event distribution.

**Type**: String
**Pattern**: ^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$

(Required) Required. Unique identifier for the consumer instance, ensuring event distribution among multiple instances.

**Type**: String
**Pattern**: ^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$

(Required) Required. Specifies the starting point for event fetching, allowing instances to resume from a precise position.

**Type**: Long
**Format**: int64

(Optional) Optional. Limits the number of events returned in one response. Helps manage processing load.

**Type**: Integer
**Default**: 100

(Optional) Optional. Time to wait for events to become available in ISO 8601 duration format. Can be decreased by the server to the session timeout of the subscription.

**Type**: String
**Pattern**: ^P(?:(\d+)Y)?(?:(\d+)M)?(?:(\d+)W)?(?:(\d+)D)?(?:T(?:(\d+)H)?(?:(\d+)M)?(?:(\d+)S)?)?$
**Default**: PT30S

request.description: Fetch a batch of events starting from a specified cursor position, supporting real-time event consumption with optional limits and timeouts.

header.description:
(Required) The time in seconds to wait before retrying the poll request.

**Type**: Integer
